title: The Springtown Archive
content: Takeshi is a traveling photographer who has documented Springtown for ten years, returning every spring to capture the town's changing face. His collection includes thousands of photographs spanning a decade, including a yearly panorama from the hill above town (same spot, same time of day) that documents seasonal and structural changes. His most famous photograph captures the Starlight Festival with autumn leaves, winter constellations, and spring lanterns in a single frame. His portraits of Springtown residents - Hana at her tea shop, Sakura with her blossoms, Sora at her telescope, Nori sorting mail, Mio organizing the festival - form an intimate visual history of the town. Takeshi never sells his Springtown photographs; he gives prints as gifts to his subjects. His work has been exhibited in galleries nationwide, but he always returns to Springtown, which he considers his true home. His photographs line the walls of Hana's tea shop and serve as the town's unofficial visual archive.
